item.title: The biggest PE loss since 2008
item.description: Thoma Bravo will lose all of the $5bn it invested in Medallia as it hands the software group to lenders

item.title: Wall Street pushes US regulators to further ease Basel capital rules
item.description: Banks press for further gains even after winning their largest lobbying victory since the financial crisis

item.title: How could Thames Water be nationalised?
item.description: Intervention by UK environment secretary has raised prospect of utility entering government’s special administration regime

item.title: BMW sounds the alarm as China squeezes Europe’s carmakers
item.description: Chinese manufacturers have grown their market share in Europe while gaining share at home

item.title: JPMorgan Chase cuts off Anthropic access for its Hong Kong staff
item.description: Bank follows Goldman Sachs in preventing use of Claude in Asian financial hub

item.title: Ships divert in hunt for fuel after Gulf conflict hits supplies
item.description: Bosses warn of continued disruption despite Iran-US deal to end war and open the strait

item.title: What is the point of Berkshire Hathaway?
item.description: It can be bold — or become an index fund

item.title: BA owner warns EU competition rules make easyJet bid ‘very difficult’
item.description: IAG chief Luis Gallego calls for overhaul of how bloc evaluates airline takeovers

item.title: HMRC broadens scrutiny of founders’ pay in company sales
item.description: Authority believes some payments may be disguised remuneration, say tax advisers

item.title: Meta’s AI gamble: Dina Powell McCormick opens door to Wall Street
item.description: Former Goldman Sachs executive explores financing once alien to Silicon Valley for $600bn infrastructure push

item.title: US ‘nuclear bros’ test America’s atomic revival
item.description: Young founders backed by Silicon Valley and Trump race to start reactors despite experts’ safety fears

item.title: UK officials explore swapping some sickness benefits for jobs coaching
item.description: Menu of support options is first policy for welfare reform to be discussed since revolt by Labour MPs last year

item.title: How Uzbekistan could liberalise its economy with a push into green energy
item.description: Will the former Soviet state liberalise its economy with a push into green energy?

item.title: HSBC fined over Australian scam failures
item.description: Bank admits inadequate protection of ‘spoofing’ victims

item.title: Turbulence at Tata delays take-off for new Air India chief
item.description: Airline hit by fatal crash, rising fuel costs and record loss is searching for its next leader

item.title: FT Financial Literacy and Inclusion Campaign
item.description: The FT invites readers to join our campaign to promote financial literacy in the UK and around the world

item.title: FirstFT: Lululemon apologises after Japanese drum controversy in China
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Mukesh Ambani’s Jio set to file for IPO, and Fed officials tilt towards rate rise

item.title: Blow to UK authorities as former Nigerian oil minister cleared in bribery case
item.description: Diezani Alison-Madueke had been accused of receiving private jet flights and use of properties in top London districts

item.title: How the oil market shrugged off the Iran crisis
item.description: Fears of summer shortages and $200 oil have been replaced by a focus on looming gluts

item.title: Anthropic boss tells G7 leaders to ‘resist the temptation to splinter’ over AI
item.description: Dario Amodei is supported by rival Sam Altman in call for international co-operation

item.title: Activist investor TCIM targets Devon Energy after landmark merger
item.description: Speculation of a looming consolidation wave mounts in the heartland of the US shale oil patch

item.title: To avoid backlash, tech giants must share their AI wealth before it’s too late
item.description: The US cannot wait for the worst job losses to hit before moving corporate and public policy in a pro-worker direction

item.title: Diageo boss Dave Lewis orders executives to cut jobs as restructuring kicks off
item.description: Group’s top team given cost-reduction targets as new CEO begins effort to revive fortunes of Johnnie Walker maker

item.title: Thoma Bravo hands Medallia to lenders in one of PE’s biggest losses
item.description: Blackstone-led consortium to take over software company and inject $150mn to help cut its debt load

item.title: Why (happy) humans still matter at work
item.description: Leaders have the power to make the future of work more enticing than just ‘machine-minding’

item.title: Trump administration to pay $765mn to scuttle offshore wind projects
item.description: Power developer Invenergy will redeploy funds into natural gas and geothermal projects in western US

item.title: Billionaire trader Alex Gerko loses tax appeal at UK Supreme Court
item.description: Top court rules traders should pay income tax on share of trading profits

item.title: AI medical tools match or surpass doctors for advice
item.description: Two health models displayed clinical value across a range of diagnostic and treatment decisions, studies show

item.title: Rheinmetall to partner with US satellite group to boost German intelligence capabilities
item.description: Tankmaker and satellite imagery company Vantor to give country’s armed forces ‘sovereign control’ of technology

item.title: US retail sales jump in sign consumers are weathering petrol shock
item.description: Data shows economy remains in robust shape as Kevin Warsh takes charge of Fed
